text.title: Email Lifecycle Architecture Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Email Lifecycle Architecture. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Map every email to one of six stages: acquisition, activation, engagement, retention, resurrection, churn. Unmapped emails are noise.
rule.rationale: Without stage mapping programs cannibalise each other; explicit map enables conflict resolution.

rule.statement: Global frequency cap per subscriber: ≤N marketing emails / week (typically 3-5). Cap enforced ACROSS programs, not per-program.
rule.rationale: Per-program caps still over-mail; global cap respects subscriber inbox.

rule.statement: Suppression list is global. Marketing-unsub suppresses subscriber from ALL marketing programs.
rule.rationale: Per-program suppression is a regulatory + reputation risk.

rule.statement: Every subscriber has a consent record: opt-in date, source (form, import, purchase), legal basis.
rule.rationale: Missing record = unprovable, treated as no consent.

rule.statement: Quarterly audit: bounce, spam-complaint, unsub rate per program. Programs above threshold pause until fixed.
rule.rationale: Deliverability tanks 60-90 days after threshold breach; quarterly audit catches drift.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-email-lifecycle-architecture.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
